description: MTP-heal protocol ARM A: bf16 full-precision CEILING (natural mtp.* head, bf16-resident weights + f32 oracle path). Battery: p1/p2/p3 prompts + 8-turn agent loop, K=[1,2,3,4], N=3, NGEN=128 (256 for agent loop). Purpose: measure MTP draft-head acceptance at FULL PRECISION as the performance ceiling before quantization.
verdict: COMPLETE - ceiling established. BF16 path works correctly (no OOM when run alone, ~45min for full battery). Agent loop acceptance high (94-100% at K=1). Per-prompt acceptance aligns with expected behavior (p3 agentic context shows highest K=1/K=2, degrades at deeper K).

description: MTP-heal protocol ARM B: NVFP4 quantized (natural embedded nextn head, NVFP4 mixed trunk + Q8_0 head). Same battery as ARM A. Purpose: measure MTP draft-head acceptance on the daily quantized checkpoint - the delta vs ARM A isolates the TRUNK-INDUCED degradation (head reads degraded hidden states).
self_consistency: 39/39 PASS (5 agent-loop rows without self-consistency field)
verdict: COMPLETE - fast path ran cleanly. Acceptance profile similar to bf16 but with measurable deltas (see delta verdict).

description: MTP-heal protocol DELTA TABLE: bf16 full-precision ceiling vs NVFP4 quant hit. The delta (ceiling minus quant) = the trunk-quantization hit on MTP drafting. Experimental design note: the NVFP4 gguf stores the MTP HEAD at Q8_0 (protected), only the TRUNK is NVFP4-mixed. The measured delta therefore isolates TRUNK-INDUCED degradation (head reads degraded hidden states) - exactly the component head re-training would heal.
key_findings: SURPRISING: p1/p2 show NVFP4 WINS at K=1 (delta -3.2%, -4.8%) and most K=2/3 cells - quant accepts BETTER than bf16 for short/medium prompts at shallow K
p3 (agentic long context): bf16 WINS strongly at K=1/K=2 (delta +14.4%, +18.8%) - the ceiling emerges in complex multi-turn contexts
K=4 deep drafts: NVFP4 consistently LOSES (-10% to -24% hit) across all prompts - quantization hurts deep speculation more than shallow
Agent loop: bf16 WINS consistently (+2.3% to +7.5% per turn, growing with context length) - self-conditioning amplifies the trunk-quality signal
Hit magnitude: ranges from -10% (quant wins) to +23.9% (ceiling wins) depending on (prompt, K) - NOT a uniform degradation
verdict: COUNTERINTUITIVE RESULT: NVFP4 trunk quantization does NOT uniformly degrade MTP acceptance. At shallow K (1-2) on short/medium prompts, quant MATCHES or BEATS the bf16 ceiling (likely due to regularization effect of quantization noise). The ceiling advantage emerges only in: (1) long agentic contexts (p3), (2) deep drafts (K=4), (3) accumulating agent loops. Head retraining ROI is CONDITIONAL - highest value for K>=3 and long-context agent workloads, near-zero for K=1-2 short prompts.
next_step: MTP-HEAL step 3: retrain the MTP head on NVFP4-trunk hidden states (heal the K=4 / p3 / agent-loop degradation). But also consider: the K=1-2 regime may NOT need healing (quant already at ceiling).
